reviews.review_text:
The new update is awful. It turns the game from a balanced challenge into a one hit and you die bullet hell that the OG game 100% was NOT.  

I was really, really looking forward to his update as the base game got too easy. But just tripling the health of everything and adding bullet spam on top of the map is not fun. The only way to win in nightmare is to go pure damage with nothing else. Boring.
Brotato is a stalwart in the survivorslike genre. Endlessly fun, it feels great to play, unlike some of its competitors that quickly become stale. Can’t wait for the next DLC!

People straight up complaining that a free "nightmare" mode is too difficult... I guess I must be one of the few people the devs haven't gotten round to putting a gun to my head, forcing me to only play that difficulty.

The ‘All Pain No Gain’ DLC adds an impressive amount of challenge to a game that’s already pretty tough if you start on level 4 or 5. Still, with some patience, the right build, and careful attention to each character’s stats, it becomes stimulating rather than frustrating. It just takes a bit of practice and getting used to the tiny maps. Give it a try.

The All Pain No Gain Update:

I'm sorry but the projectiles that just appear from the sides of the screen are just a cheesy mechanic to artificially make the game more difficult. I want a harder challenge the update bring but I'm not a fast twitch bullet hell gamer (I'm 54) so this mechanic currently just ruins my enjoyment of a great game. 

If I wanted a bullet-hell style of game I'd go play enter the gungeon or similar.

If Blob Fish games want to have projectiles, then put stationary monsters around the edge of the screen with high health that fire them. Then the player at least has a choice to try and kill them to get rid of the projectiles.

The 'dark mode' is good and has interactions with objects in game which is a great design.

I've "perfected" this game on three different occasions because every time they release an update there are new achievements.  Neat! I get to play a game I enjoy.

Except now, whoever is a sadist on the dev team, please fire them.  This new nightmare mode is absolutely infuriating.  The game is no longer fun.  At least on danger 5 I had fun.  Nightmare is just; what the ♥♥♥♥. I actually have to uninstall this so I don't yeet my steam deck across the room.

Brotato spuds. One of the most fun roguelites I’ve played. The potato theme could’ve been a gimmick, but it’s baked into the charm of the game. Every weird little spud character feels distinct and pushes you toward different builds, weapon combos, and synergies. One run you’re a speedy glass-cannon potato slicing aliens into au gratin, the next you’re an unkillable mashed tank going full hasselback with six flamethrowers. Maybe next you’re a big old pig of a spud, just windmilling around the arena.

The gameplay loop is crisp, chaotic, and very replayable. Unlocking new taters and experimenting with builds keeps things farm fresh. Highly recommended for all roguelite fans and, of course, potato enthusiasts. A must-play if you like potatoes and roguelites. Peak tuber gaming.

this is a co-op masterpiece up there with Overcooked and above Nuclear Throne. my wife and I both love this game. the balance between strategic shopping and intense battles is surprisingly genius. took us around 22 hours to beat Danger 5. the wealth of different creative characters and items will keep this game alive in our library for a very long time.

I am very certain that no person is playtesting their updates since the Nightmare difficulty should be very hard, but it feels more stupid than hard. 

Brotato managed to go from many builds that get fewer the higher the difficulty gets and difficulty 5 only allowed a hand full of combos on every character and Nightmare feels like you almost no build variation, cementing on true meta for every character.

Brotato managed to inspire Noobs are coming, to make an awesome game and the feel they have in their game, way suprasses everything Brotato ever tried to achieve

I would still recommend Brotato, but the more time you invest into it, the worse it gets.

Great game, I love playing even though it is difficult with some characters. I never get tired of trying to figure out how to win and it has improved my overall skills in general. I love how  my brotato changes with the items. Hopefully I will get my skill up for the DLC it looks like a lot of fun. 10/10 this game is fun great to look at and has my soul

700 hours played.

I used to recommend Brotato to everyone. The game was fun because you could experiment, break the game with crazy builds, and enjoy the chaos in endless mode.

The No Pain No Gain update and Nightmare difficulty completely changed that feeling for me. Instead of rewarding creativity and strong builds, the game now feels exhausting and overly punishing.

I honestly don’t understand who this update was made for. I never saw players asking for the game to become frustrating.

For the first time since buying Brotato, I’m not even sure I want to continue playing.

Before the All Pain No Gain update, I really would have recommended the game. Yes, there were some balancing issues with the shop being bloated and it being difficult getting the items you want. But winning a run was possible. Since the update, I have tried a lot of times to even get passed wave 12. The new Nightmare difficulty is unbalanced and unfun. And whilst I enjoy challenging games, this update has pushed Brotato into the category of the devs never playing the game themselves and realising how insanely difficult they have made it.

After the last update, "Nightmare mode" completely ruined what made Brotato fun for me. The game used to feel challenging but fair — now it just feels overtuned and frustrating for the sake of difficulty.

Enemy scaling is absurd, elites become damage sponges, and half the runs feel RNG-dependent instead of skill-based. A lot of builds that were viable before are now useless unless you abuse a tiny handful of meta weapons/items. It went from a chaotic roguelike where experimentation was rewarding to “play this exact build or lose.”

Performance also feels worse during later waves with all the extra effects and enemies on screen. Add in some questionable balance changes and it honestly feels like the update was never properly tested.

Nightmare mode should be hard, not exhausting. Right now it feels more annoying than fun.

Trash game 2/10... Hope the next update will not be this bad, like this one.

Stupid update. The new developers really want to make people suffer (all pain no gain update). They didn't have any new ideas but really wanted to undermine the armor+dodge mechanic in this game. So they decided that throwing 100s of projectiles at the player that do billion damage each was the only thing missing in the game. All this does is make shop rng much more important and people can't have fun playing the non-strong potatoes. 

Not every game has to be a souls game, let us have our relaxed time. If you don't have any new ideas, stop updating the game.

TL;DR: I say this as someone who has cleared nightmare with multiple potatoes. The new update sucks. The developers don't understand what made this game fun.
They keep making it more interesting, adding new mechanics and items. It's pretty fun.
It is so good and fun, it is a turn yout brain off type game so you can watch a show while playing

Amazing action roguelike, hack and slash game with alot of replayability due to the difficulty options.
Keeps getting free updates and DLCs which is great.
Coop mode with friends is even more fun even though I have to admit the new highest difficulty seems to be a bit unfinished in terms of scaling for multiplayer.
Overall great game, can only advise to get this game especially if you look for a nice coop game with friends via steam remote play.
Epic soundtracks by the way, turn up the music if you play :D
very fun game it ran susprisingly well considoring my specs

Has pretty handily crossed a line into the "games that are not fun to keep up with" category for me.

The game is fun enough on it's own, but I really played it to chase achievements, and up until now there weren't any achievements that felt completely out of my reach. But for some reason the devs felt that the game needed an ultra hardcore, CBT, only play if you're a REAL GAMER™ mode and I can't say I'd enjoy trying to grind that out.
An awesome game! The updates are very good, many quality of life stuff
I really love this game, but playing with friends isnt really enjoyable since therer are connection issues all the time. Why cant the developers implement a normal multiplayer mode?

Adapting your strategy for every single character is an absolute blast. The real fun starts when your build finally clicks and you push way past wave 20. Highly recommended if you love a good challenge!
Just play the game. I won't spoil anything for you. I'll just say that the game is worth more than it's listed for.

[h1]One of the best Potato games of all time[/h1]

[h2]The Good[/h2]
[list]
    [*]Gameplay is simple and easy to understand, yet through the vast selection of weapons, items and characters, there's so much content in a small little box
    [*]Some of the characters are REALLY unique and warp playstyles completely, like [spoiler]damaging yourself as your main damaging tool[/spoiler] or [spoiler]not killing enemies at all[/spoiler]
    [*]It's not super intensive as other roguelikes, and can be quite relaxing to play, which all depends on the difficulty settings
    [*]QOL settings and features like able to retry a wave, locking items and banning items, are all just nice to have
[/list]

[h2]The Bad[/h2]
[list]
    [*]Some people may find the space very dull, since the level, for the most part, is just an empty box (even with the DLC map), main interaction is with the enemies and items
[/list]

[h2]The Random[/h2]
[list]
    [*]Characters are not balanced, which really isn't an issue, but some characters are just significantly harder to get going than others
    [*]Because of the super wide variety of items, there's bound to be a couple that i just have never picked up, unless its a very very specific character/build im using
[/list]

[h2]Overall Thoughts[/h2]
It's a simple roguelike, with a lot of different playstyles, that is quite addictive, and can keep you playing for way more hours than you would think

Spend some time to perfect the game but the devs just won't let you have it. New content is not changing anything, it's just more of the same quite repetitive gameplay that's fun for a while but you have to know your game limits.

The "All pain no gain" update made the game far less fun. The new difficulty puts way too much emphasis on getting the perfect spread of stats and economy, killing the viability of many items and runs. "Hard" can be fun, but this new "Danger 6" is just frustration.

Pros:
- Addictive gameplay loop
- low price point
- game music isnt irritating
Cons:
- I feel the game content, although appearing extensive, lacks depth
- The game difficulty is focused on keeping your head above water with upgrades throughout each run, rather than skill. IE you get better at the game via an understanding of the game upgrade formula rather than skill.

Retarded latest update greatly higlights how incompetent EE with brotato, thats what i've been affraid of when this "extended life support" dropped. While YES new difficulty is tottaly "optional", the fact that someone over at EE really thought that "yeah nightmare is tottaly fine". Taking the worst aspects of the game and multiplying them x10 is just idk .... bravo. Have ANY ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ living soul at EE playtested this difficulty? Have someone thought "how would bull play this" or "how melee will play this"? I fear that the answer is NO.   Would like to stop here to not waste time for other questionable moves they make.

Just beat the game and wanted to give my honest thoughts on it. First I'm going to straight up say that I enjoyed it throughout; Brotato is a wonderful example of the "pick up and play" concept, especially on a Steam Deck. It has an addicting, tight gameplay loop with lots of upgrades and quick waves. There is a large emphasis on shop purchases between rounds, ultimately affecting your builds in crazy & creative ways. [b][u]However, there are some flaws to consider before getting into it.[/u][/b]

Currently, much of the playerbase isn't too pleased with the "All Pain, No Gain" update, specifically a new difficulty setting. When playing on it, the game turns into a pretty exhausting and RNG-dependent experience. The scaling is extremely punishing; enemies are so overtuned that certain characters cannot win. It eliminates build variety and just isn't fun. I wish it were designed as a great challenge but one where you're rewarded for overcoming seemingly insurmountable odds.

My point is that the game is pivoting in a different direction, increasing the devs' workload for all the future balancing changes. It feels as if the formula we all love was unnecessarily tweaked, and a new precedent is being set where this behavior is seemingly ok. Hopefully that isn't the case.

Another gripe is the achievements, since this game does revolve around beating challenges. [i]Each update brings more of them.[/i] For any completionists out there, bear mind that a 100% requires clearing both the main and its DLC [b]twice[/b] with every character. A new, second DLC is coming out soon that will very likely increase said workload. Can definitely see why it might feel like a slog unlocking everything at later stages.

Despite these flaws, the game has lots of QoL accessibility features to tinker with: number of enemies, enemy HP & speed, turning off environmental projectiles, retrying waves after failure, etc. The punishing difficulty setting mentioned earlier is also completely optional and does not change the entire game around it, save for two unlocks. Worried about the item bloat? Ban them in the shop.

In conclusion, I think Brotato merits a spotlight on its own compared to other "survivor-like" games. It's surprisingly full of depth, encourages ample experimentation, has a personality, and is cheap to buy. Recommend taking it slow and not rush through; appreciate it for what it is.

gets boring pretty quickly but it's good enough to zone out and kill time
wouldn't play again
nightmare difficulty is not fun at all and disgusting. If you have not completed yourself, don't release the update!

It was pure fun until the last update No Pain No Gain, or more like No Fun All Pain. What's the point of introducing mechanics that make the game entirely RNG-based outside of meta builds? If that's the way this game is heading I think it's gonna die like many frustrating bullet hells. That's it, I said it - Brotato has become a bullet hell, which it was not before and that was the reason why it was so fun to play. At this point, if you want to try this game just reckon with the fact, that at some point gameplay becomes idiotic and frustatring beyond the point of tolerance.

Its amazing you can shoot baby aliens and stuff 
its fun until you die at wave 20 when youve been playing for an hour
but you play as a brotato so its funny 
thank

I can't believe I almost spent 60 hours of my life watching an evermore monstrous potato blow blob things to bits for cash.

The game excels at making the loop and decision points just right. Avoiding becoming a slog while also keeping it fresh by throwing hundreds of well-balanced (!) options at you. And that is a rare feat in this crowded genre space.

This game is very well designed and poses a fun challenge for many hours. I still haven't even scratched the surface when it comes to getting everything and I'm still having a blast. I would definitely recommend this game to anyone looking for a fun time.
I adore this game. I've sunk many hours into it. But for the second time the game has cashed mid play and wiped all of my data. I'm done.

What a great game! I've been a fan of Brotato for quite awhile, but the new nightmare mode has really solidified my appreciation for everyone working on this project. This game has always been great at forcing the player to learn and adapt to the various obstacles that come with each danger, and rewards them for doing so. Nightmare mode is no exception. Not to mention, this game is really fun to play with my wife. The couch co-op feature works seamlessly and has caused Brotato to become one of our favorite games. Excited for Primal Dread!
its super fun and easy to play, would recommend for casual gamers
not much to say, one of the best arena shooter out there, new updates, new dlc's - this game will never die
a fun game to play by yourself, or even with the family, i would really recommend this game for others.

its the best game for the "don't know what to game" mood.

But honestly its even better than that. The game hits a spot i cant describe. The fast passing and the constant mini decisions that will ultimately lead to you being a god-like potato hits different.

Absolutely amazing game and you just need one hand (even one finger is enough) to play it! The progression system with just unlocking new items and characters is spot-on. It’s fun to play solo, but couch co-op takes it to a whole new level (letting you pull off some absolutely wild character combos). This is by far one of my favorite games for the Steam Deck!
Very fun and addictive game loop
This game scratches an itch that no other game seems to really come close to. Great 30ish minute sessions, easily pausable, and every character is winnable even if it takes a bunch of tries.

Lol'deki eşya ve güç mantığının daha zevkli bir versiyonuyla işliyor, eşya alma kısmında deneyebileceğiniz sonsuz taktik var ama savaş anında biraz otomatiğe kaçmış,çünkü alan çok kalabalık düşmanlarla dolu ve sürekli tarıyorsunuz, bunun yerine daha az ama dayanıklı düşmanlarla savaşarak mağazadan aldığımız eşyaların işlevini savaş anında görebilsek bence çok daha iyi olurdu
very fun to play and a lot of unique characters to complete
So much replay value, I would have liked more items in the base game, but overall a lot of fun. I will say some characters at level 5 feel borderline impossible to win.

After roughly 6 hours i Beat my first run. What an amazing game. Planning and building synergies is important here unlike in a lot of games in this genre. There's a lot of classes, weapons, and items to play around with. Well worth the price!

I love everything about this game.
The weapons, characters and above all the Local Coop Feature, it is a peak friend group activity to play this game with 3 people on one device and its just perfect for that 
Perfomance wise it does not get laggy until very VERY late rounds and its suited for almost every kind of hardwear.
10/10
super fun and lots of build options IYKYK
It's something you can do if you're bored and looking to grind out some things, a little repetitive yes but then again, so is every game to a certain point

Great fun, as Gaming Dad Legends points out, easy to pick up and play. But there's just enough to keep you going.
Gameplay escalates at a good lick, theres enough sense of impact to the combat. Silly fun
It's challenging and engaging. I enjoy playing solo and multiplayer

Great game, I love playing it.  Great to kill some time without having to get to invested in the story or a multi player game only.  Progression is good, varied characters good, and overall would love to see a vr game for this just for the horror aspect.
Amazing game with brilliant scaling and gameplay

I have spent too much time on this game. It's got a cute visual aesthetic paired with simple controls, a nice reminder that a good game doesn't need the backing of a huge company or team to produce. It really just has to be fun.

A very addictive game, Brotato has one fatal flaw: Most satisfying builds don't feel "complete" until close to Wave 20 or even after, and the game is not balanced at all for Endless Mode. For example, even if your damage mitigation is over 99% and you have insane amounts of life and recovery, monster burst damage can easily wipe you off the map after Wave 25. This encourages (nay, requires) a meta-game that forces you to go all in on offense, because a dead monster is no threat to you. (Cf. Path of Exile.) That eliminates the subtle dance of balancing offense and defense and cheapens the overall experience.

Overall, I can recommend this game for the diversity of play styles it presents, but I really wish the latter part of each wave weren't so broken. Alternatively, having waves end later (and remain balanced to that point) would allow players to flesh out their builds more before your play with that build suddenly ends (if not playing Endless Mode) or your defensive decisions all become invalidated (if playing Endless Mode).
One of those games I feel I'm still trash at... Even after completing it on a mac, pc, ps5, steamdeck and ios.
A lot of characters and massive replay ability!!! Highly addictive!

I think this qualifies as a rogue-like (not rogue-lite).  Each game you start with a "hero" and fight the swarm comprised of weak, strong, or fast melee and ranged units with the occasional boss or two thrown in.  Now with six difficulty levels, a variety of enemies, and dozens of "hero" spuds with various buffs or nerfs, there is a huge variety in gameplay.  Although the RNG is frustrating when none of the options match your build, the difficulty makes the wins feel better than playing on easy.  Technically the game is easy to pickup and play for a few minutes at a time, but the loop of trying again for better RNG keeps me locked in for hours.  Runs great on Steam Deck, too.
I have this game on PC, PS5 and Nintendo Switch. It somehow never gets old, and is super fun in co-op mode.
This is a devilishly fun game where it totally sucks your time away.  You start to play and next thing you know hours have passed you by.
Best $5 I've ever spent. It's a solid bullet hell game that now has couch co-op, plus the graphics are hilarious and they're consistently maintaining it and coming out with new expansions.
It's fun to play while listening to podcasts, music, or something else in the background.
I'm having a blast all the way through and the high difficulty makes the game very replayable.

Been playing since the BETA. Brotato is an amazing game that has such an interesting macro, it will keep you engaged for hours. Replay-ability to the MAX; not to mention the Soundtrack is god tier and works perfectly with this game. Cannot get any better than Brotato. Big thanks to the devs for polishing this game into a full-fledged piece of art.
10/10 game. Who doesnt like a game about potatos fighting aliens(with dlc that can also be fish or the upcomming dinos)

Amazing roguelite, runs great on the deck and has an insane amount of characters, weapons, items to unlock and use. Loads of different builds to try out and create. Music is also surprisingly good! Just play this game :)
Be careful... highly addictive and absolute fun.
Can become addictive very easily. With friends it's goated but even solo it can be a challenge if you try to play all kinds of characters.

Brotato is proof that sometimes all a game really needs is a potato with attitude, six weapons, and absolutely no respect for personal space.

On paper, it sounds ridiculous.

You are a potato.
You are in a small arena.
Things are trying to kill you.
You respond by becoming increasingly armed, increasingly weird, and eventually a biological war crime with eyebrows.

And somehow, it works beautifully.

At first I thought, “Right, this is silly.” Then I blinked and I was deeply invested in whether my angry little starch goblin should carry six sticks, six guns, six medical devices, or some horrifying combination of all three. Brotato does that dangerous thing where it looks simple enough to play with half your brain, but then quietly asks the other half to start doing build maths like you are preparing a tactical briefing for a root vegetable insurgency.

The runs are short, the upgrades come quickly, and every character changes things just enough to make you think, “I’ll try one more.” This is, of course, a lie. “One more run” in Brotato means you will look up later and realise time has passed, your tea is cold, and you have developed strong opinions about attack speed, harvesting, dodge chance, and whether a potato should be allowed to hold six flamethrowers.

The answer is yes. Obviously.

It is casual enough to jump into after work, but it has enough depth that you can absolutely convince yourself you are not wasting time, you are conducting important research into violence, economy, and tuber-based resilience. Some characters feel powerful. Some feel cursed. Some feel like the developer looked at balance, smiled politely, and pushed it down a flight of stairs.

And I respect that.

Brotato is cheap, quick, addictive, weirdly tactical, and much better than a game about a heavily armed potato has any right to be. It is not trying to be noble. It is not trying to change your life. It is simply handing you a potato, a weapon, and a problem.

Who doesn’t love a potato with an attitude?

You have been warned.
Was unimpressed at first coming from Vampire Survivors but once I began to understand the mechanics I prefer this game much more than VS. It's harder to break the game but so satisfying once you do.
Great game,  Friends still come over every now and then to play together.
Honestly, most fun Vampire Survivors-like I’ve ever played, including the genre’s namesake. Lots of characters, weapons, and items give the game a ton of replayability.

Let me start off by saying I play this game mostly with my buddies on his xbox so my in game hours are not correct in the slightest. This game is like crack ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ cocaine in the gaming world, so many variables and different ways to play this fast paced adrenaline inducing ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. We all know ghost number one potato when i pull that 90% dodge and just non stop gamba for my life I feel like a 70 year old grandma sitting at a slot machine in los vegas gambling away my grandchildrens college fund. Some people would think how good could a rougelike potato game really be and my answer to that is sit down and shut the ♥♥♥♥ up and play. 10/10 possibly best game ever made we always wonder what the DaVinci or Mozart of our time will be I would argue it's brotato maybe I'm hooked like a fent addict on the streets of california but I would argue I'm correct. In conclusion if you don't play this game you've missed out on life and should possibly reconsider your life choices. Possibly go back to college? Possibly toutch grass? I say nay thou shall play brotato!

Potato
Why?
Why not?
Not in a bad way but this game is text book definition of ROGUELIKE
go in, go out, retry better or worse, eventually you are going to meet the end goal,
Probably in 15 years I will be completing this game (But that's skill issue and we will ignore it)

this game is very fun i like it a lot it is very active with updates and dlc's and i  am looking forward to the new dlc coming soon all of the weapons are all good and unique in there own way and there is at least one that will fit what your going for in your run

It’s simple, fast, and really addictive. You pick a potato, grab weapons, survive waves, buy upgrades, and slowly turn your build into something ridiculous. Every run feels different because there are so many characters, weapons, and item combos to mess around with.

The best part is how quick everything is. You don’t need a big setup or long tutorial. You just jump in, start killing enemies, and keep tweaking your build between waves. It’s easy to understand, but there’s still a lot of strategy once you start chasing harder difficulties.

At first i was unsure about this game.
It is simple but also deep. lots of fun and many hours of play.

First one of these vampire survivor games I've played; usually more into strategy games (civ, sts against the storm), but this has been a lot of fun for the $5 i paid for it. Not too deep, but the number of characters with their specific traits is a lot of fun to play around with.

A legendary indie game that created its own sub genre. Often imitated, never surpassed. A ridiculous, gigantic amount of characters and options, all surprisingly balanced and well tuned, with unique gimmicks and differing play styles, if of greatly differing levels of challenge. Another one of those worshiped, glorfiied indie games that really is as good as they hype suggests. If you have any love for this genre and you can tolerate the harder than average difficulty this game is an absolute no brainer of a purchase that will give far more than it costs even if not bought on sale. I actually do hope someone takes Brotatos formula and makes something even better, but 3 years later it hasn't happened. ANd if you're still not sure it has a free demo on itch.io.

This is not merely good or even excellent, its outright legendary.

This is quite possibly the greatest rogue survivor game I've ever played, the only one even remotely in the same ballpark being Vampire Survivors
Super addicting, runs exceptionally well on the deck. Great for winding down at the end of the day when you dont want to think and just nerd up
Very fun and strategic, its a bit overwhelming at the start but once you go through a couple runs its a lot easier to manage.

Honestly, I can't stop playing this game. If you like roguelikes or bullet heavens, this is a must-have.
The good stuff: Insane build variety, tons of unique characters, and fast-paced gameplay that feels super rewarding.
It's the perfect "just one more run" game.
10 hours in and I'm already hooked. Absolutely worth it!
Very good game, worth the money. Fun and tactical.
Fun game worth every penny. This is the perfect right before bed detoxing kind of game.
very repetitive, had no fun after 10 hours or so. there is also barely any variety in this game as you will have to stick to one or two builds to win at higher difficulties

Love this. Great swarm game with heaps of weapons, upgrades, builds, ect.
It is kinda annoying that more steam achievements keep getting added though.

If you want something face paced, easy to pick up and put down, and a great couch co-op game- look no further. The music is fire and gameplay is super easy to learn! 100+ hours in so far and no end in sight.
Such an easy game to recommend. Endless fun and co-op is equally amazing.

One of the few good shared screen local coop games, I appreciate this game a lot.
Great to see that the new devs put effort into keeping it alive.

A survivor style roguelike with an insane amount of build diversity. Since most items have some sort of tradeoff from acquiring it makes the decisions a lot more thought out. You don't just play a few games until you unlock starting damage and extra lives and then never struggle again.

Tired of doom scrolling? Come rot your brain on roguelite gambling.

It’s slots but for skills (weapons and items). 

Have a bad PC? That’s ok sell it and buy this game. You could literally have a potato and play this game.

Devs that constantly updates and add content for a game this cheap is worth supporting.

Devs keep adding more and more content to a quite cheap game and I'm enjoying myself.  I just think we need more VS clones that dont have 30 minute runtimes that force you to pick your upgrades carefully or you DIE.

Kinda crazy seeing people with 100+ hours leave a recent negative review because of increased difficulty.

You can simply, not play, the new difficulty released and enjoy lower difficulties if you don't enjoy the challenge.
Nice repatitive gameplay. The game have a slow start but once you learn how it works it just flows.
Brotato is one of the best rougelikes on steam, the replayability, strategies, and builds are near infinite, all for a cheap price. 10/10 would reccomend.

You look more like an egg, but whatever. I reached danger 5 with most characters playing on XBOX (200 hr), and because reasons, I purchased Brotato to play on my non-gaming PC, and have reached Danger 5 with some characters. I like this game. I recommend the game if you like these types. There are numerous ways to build your eggtato, with new unlocks as you progress.  Learned the best defense is more offense, most of the time.  

Downsides it can be tough and frustrating at times.. But the celebration when you finally overcome that struggle. I do get annoyed when I can't get  HP upgrades at level ups - even after re-rolling all my coins away.  Sometimes  you get a weapon early and it never shows up again.. Its not a perfect game, but I've still played a combined 250+ hrs. I'm not addicted.. dont say that.
One of my favorite games. I keep coming back to play it. The item combinations and tight controls feel like its always my fault if I lose.
It will get ya.  More weapons, more trees, more everything.  Over...and over....and over.......
This game absolutely one-shot me. 200 hours in I'm still playing! 10/10 would (will) play for another 200.
